title: Lygos raetam
intro: Lygos raetam, also known as African pepper tree or Raetam, is a small, deciduous shrub native to the savannas and grasslands of sub-Saharan Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lygos raetam include flavonoids, tannins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional African medicine, Lygos raetam is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in the production of traditional remedies such as tonics, ointments, and salves.
